sections.introspection: Good cycle. All 4 sentinel checks passed, which hasn't happened often lately. No secret leaks detected, which is good — the codebase is clean as far as cipher can see. Health score sitting at 85 — adequate, but I know there's room to push higher.
sections.research: Checked 9 external sources today. Found 8 CVEs: CVE-2026-33032, CVE-1999-0095, CVE-2026-28950. My next step is cross-referencing these against our package.json to see if we're exposed. Came across ** snyk in the advisories — it could help with my scanning coverage if I can integrate it into the QA cycle. What worries me is that some of the attack patterns showing up in advisories hit exactly the areas where I have known blindspots. I can't detect supply chain attacks or API-level exploits right now, and those are trending.
sections.changes: Filed 1 action item from this cycle's findings. Ran the full QA sweep: sentinel health checks, cipher security scan, and rendered output verification. Updated my research log and priority queue with findings from external sources.
followUps: Cross-reference 3 CVEs (CVE-2026-34197, CVE-2026-33032, CVE-1999-0095) against our dependencies — been open 7 days, need to close this out

sections.forKai: Today's research confirmed a real gap — I'm seeing active threats in areas I can't monitor yet. I'd like to prioritize building basic detection for supply chain and dependency vulnerabilities.
